LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JUSTIN FRANCE'S MOTION FOR ORDER TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS GRANTED AND THE COURT WILL ENTER JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO THE TERMS OF THE SETTLEMENT. PURSUANT TO PARAGRAPH 13 OF THE PARTIES' SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT, PLAINTIFF MAY SEEK AN AWARD OF ITS REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS INCURRED IN CONNECTION WITH THIS MOTION. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IN AN ATTEMPT TO RESOLVE PLAINTIFF'S ENTITLEMENT TO REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO CIV. CODE 1794(D) AND PARAGRAPH 3(E) OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT.(AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S NOTES/TRANSCRIPT). ORDER AND JUDGMENT ARE SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER AND JUDGMENT FOR DETAILS). JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED; DEPT. 302.

calendar.matters:
Plaintiff Justin France Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Enforcing The Settlement And For An Order Amending The Judgment (Part 2 Of 2 For Tentative Ruling Purposes Only)
Off Calendar Oct-15-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Plaintiff Justin France Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Enforcing The Settlement And For An Order Amending The Judgment (Part 2 Of 2) is off calendar as a duplicate entry for purposes of entry of the tentative ruling only. (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Enforcing The Settlement And For An Order Amending The Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-15-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JORDAN SANNIPOLI, Esq. of The Bickel Law Firm, Inc. (Tel: 619-374-4100) for Plaintiff Justin France;
Atty. JON UNIVERSAL, Esq. of Universal & Shannon, LLP (Tel: 916-780-4050) for Defendant Mercedes-Benz USA, LLC.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JUSTIN FRANCE'S Motion For An Order Enforcing The Settlement And For An Order Amending The Judgment.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling:

Plaintiff Justin France's motion for an order enforcing settlement and amending judgment is denied in its entirety. Defendant is awarded its attorney's fees in the amount of $2,300 for responding to Plaintiff's meritless motion.

On October 3, 2019, this Court granted plaintiff's motion for an order under section 664.6 of the Code of Civil Procedure enforcing the settlement agreement between the parties, and entered judgment pursuant to the terms of the settlement agreement, ordering defendant to pay $54,230.14 to plaintiff and pay off plaintiff's loan on the subject vehicle in the amount of $102,727.36, with any overpayment on the loan being refunded directly to plaintiff. On January 29, 2020, Defendant complied with that judgment by paying Plaintiff the ordered amount of restitution and by paying Plaintiff's lender the payoff amount of $102,727.26, and Plaintiff subsequently received a refund check. (Youssefi Decl. pars. 9, 13.) Thereafter, on March 17, 2020, the court granted plaintiff's motion for payment of attorney's fees and reimbursement of costs and expenses in the amount of $58,355.00 in attorneys' fees and $2,019.37 in costs and expenses. Defendant paid the ordered fees and costs on August 12, 2020. (Youssefi Decl. par. 32.)

The Court rejects Plaintiff's request that the Court amend the judgment to "clarify" the amount of overpayments to be refunded to include payments that Plaintiff supposedly made both before and after the settlement agreement was entered into on March 8, 2019. The Court sustains Defendant's evidentiary objections to the Youssefi Declaration on grounds of lack of foundation and hearsay. Plaintiff makes no admissible showing that the refund check he received did not accurately reflect the amount of overpayments due him or that Defendant has not fully complied with this Court's judgment and orders. Section 664.6 does not authorize a court to add to or modify an express term of a settlement agreement. (Leeman v. Adams Extract & Spice, LLC (2015) 236 Cal.App.4th 1367, 1374-1375.)

This Court having already entered judgment in this action pursuant to the terms of the settlement agreement, Plaintiff's motion to enforce the settlement agreement is moot. Likewise, Plaintiff offers no colorable basis for amending the judgment to reflect the amount of the Court's March 17 order awarding attorney's fees and costs. That order is independently enforceable, and Defendant has already complied with it. The Court rejects Plaintiff's belated request that it award post-judgment interest on amounts that have already been paid in full. Defendant is entitled to its attorney's fees in the amount of $2,300.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

MOTION - Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs Motion For Payment Of Attorneys Fees And Reimbursement Of Costs And Expenses
Off Calendar Mar-17-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JUSTIN FRANCE'S MOTION FOR PAYMENT OF ATTORNEYS FEES AND REIMBURSEMENT OF COSTS AND EXPENSES.

Appearances: Jordan K. Sannipoli of the Bickel Law Firm, Inc. (619.374.4100) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff and moving party; Adam Khan of Universal & Shannon, LLP (916.780.4050) appearing via Court Call for defendant Mercedes-Benz USA, LLC.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: granted in the amount of $58,355.00 in attorney's fees and $2,019.37 in costs and expenses.

The court rejects defendant's contention that it should significantly reduce plaintiff's fee request. Defendant's position is disingenuous and inconsistent with the record. First, while defendant contends that counsel's hourly rates are excessive, plaintiff's moving papers show that those rates are reasonable, and defendant has not presented any evidence to rebut that showing. Second, defendant asserts that the court should not award "fees on fees" because a motion for attorney's fees was supposed to be the "last resort, not another billing event." However, defendant does not dispute plaintiff's showing that on six different occasions before a settlement agreement was finalized, plaintiff sought to negotiate fees and costs but was rebuffed: defendant never made an offer. (Sannipoli Decl. 12-13, 15-16, 20-24, 27, Exs. D-E, G-I.) Further, defendant makes no showing that it ever sought to comply with the court's directive in its October 3, 2019 order that it meet and confer with plaintiff in an attempt to resolve plaintiff's entitlement to a reasonable fee award. Third, defendant asserts that the court should cut off plaintiff's fees as of February 21, 1998, the date of its section 998 offer, contending that there was "no need to litigate this case beyond that." However, the record shows that it was defendant's own foot-dragging that forced plaintiff to continue litigating, to the point that in September 2019, plaintiff was forced to file a motion to enforce the settlement agreement that the parties had reached in March 2019-a development as to which defendant's opposition papers are conspicuously silent. "Where a party continues to litigate after receiving a settlement offer, absent a finding that failure to resolve the case through negotiation was unreasonable or solely attributable to counsel's desire to generate more fees, additional fees incurred to establish liability or damages, including evidence of willfulness necessary to recover civil penalties, are properly included in an award of fees under Civil Code section 1794, subdivision (d)." (Hanna v. Mercedes-Benz USA, LLC (2019) 36 Cal.App.5th 493, 508 [rejecting identical argument by same defendant, represented by same counsel].) For similar reasons, the court rejects defendant's contention that a significant portion of the claimed fees were not reasonably incurred. (Continued to Part 2)

The court declines to award a positive multiplier. (See Hall v. FCA US LLC (E.D. Cal. May 21, 2018) 2018 WL 2298431 at *8 [declining to award multiplier in Song-Beverly Act case where, among other things, case did not present novel or difficult questions of law or fact, there was no evidence that the nature of the litigation precluded other employment by the attorneys, and the contingent nature of the fee award was outweighed by the other factors]; Steel v. GMC (D.N.J. 1995) 912 F.Supp. 724, 746 ["the issues in lemon law litigation are not complex and do not require a significant amount of legal analysis or novel pleading"].) Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, (E: maria.torreano@gmail.com) (302/EPS)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-13-2019 Off Calendar

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Enforce Settlement Agreement; Memo Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Oct-03-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Amara Sarah Turzak,Esq. 619-374-4100 Bickel Law Firm, Inc. for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Jon Universal, Esq. 916-780-4050 for Defendant through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF JUSTIN FRANCE'S Motion For Order To Enforce Settlement Agreement.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiff's motion to enforce the settlement agreement is granted. The parties do not dispute that the settlement agreement was in writing with the parties' signatures. (See Code Civ. Proc., 664.6.) A settlement agreement is interpreted according to the same principles as any other written agreement. (Leeman v. Adams Extract & Spice, LLC (2015) 236 Cal.App.4th 1367, 1374 ["If the language of the agreement is clear and explicit and does not involve an absurdity, determination of the mutual intent of the parties and interpretation of the contract is to be based on the language of the agreement alone."].)

Defendant argues the settlement agreement should be voided or, in the alternative, reformed, due to an alleged mistake of fact or fraud. However, the Court finds the settlement agreement to be clear and unambiguous. Further, the settlement agreement contains an integration clause which provides that there are no prior or contemporaneous understandings or agreements of the parties. As the record reflects, the parties, both represented by counsel, negotiated for an entire year after the section 998 offer to compromise. (Khan Decl., 3; Turzak Decl., Ex. A.) In the parties' efforts to compromise, the agreed-upon settlement figure was negotiated down from $81,593.88 to the eventual agreed-upon payment of $54,230.14. (Khan Decl., Ex. B.) Defendant had ample opportunity to verify the figures and conduct its own investigation, and indeed admits that if it had simply noted the dates of the four contested payments, it would have realized that they were made prior to Plaintiff leasing the subject vehicle. (Opp. at 5.) Defendant has not shown that the agreement is unconscionable or unduly unfavorable to it. Donovan v. RRL Corp. (2001) 26 Cal.4th 261 is distinguishable as it involved a contract between a consumer and car dealership, where the enforcement of the contract would be unconscionable in light of the magnitude of the parties' mistake. (Id.) Here, the agreement was negotiated by counsel over the period of one year, resulting in a compromise and a signed settlement agreement. Defendant does not provide any evidence that the calculated payments in the settlement agreement are erroneous or were anything but the product of careful negotiations and thoughtful compromise.

Plaintiff's motion to enforce the settlement agreement is granted, and the court will enter judgment pursuant to the terms of the settlement. Pursuant to paragraph 13 of the parties' settlement agreement, Plaintiff may seek an award of its reasonable attorney's fees and costs incurred in connection with this motion. The parties are ordered to meet and confer in an attempt to resolve Plaintiff's entitlement to reasonable attorney's fees and costs pursuant to Civ. Code 1794(d) and paragraph 3(E) of the settlement agreement.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order and Judgment are signed. (see scanned order and judgment for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
